campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall budget status. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, such as better connections between different types of transit and pedestrian-friendly routes.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and money management
City budget and treasurer's financial report
New multimodal transportation plan to improve buses, biking, and walking routes

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community feedback on a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipping the usual first reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance will be voted on for final approval at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident input
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for approval
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading requirement waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve the official records from several meetings held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting primarily focused on cleaning up administrative paperwork and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved meeting minutes from six March 2026 sessions
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given approval to execute contract terms

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 15, 2026 to prepare for upcoming council sessions. The council reviewed and finalized the agenda for their April 21st meeting, made decisions about which items needed specific time slots, and reviewed the draft agenda for April 28th. They also received an update on public records from early April and heard from the VEBA Advisory Committee.
keyTopics: Finalized April 21, 2026 council meeting agenda
Scheduled time slots for specific agenda items
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026
Prepared draft agenda for April 28, 2026 meeting
VEBA Advisory Committee report

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a planning meeting to review Mountain View's spending plans for building and improving city infrastructure over the next year. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget for city construction and infrastructure projects
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review previous meeting notes and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new ordinance that would update the city's residential tenant protections program.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New ordinance to update tenant protection rules (Chapter 19.71 of Municipal Code)
Residential tenant protections program changes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents had the opportunity to speak about issues for up to 3 minutes each. The council also held elections for chair and vice chair positions and discussed the city's budget for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Public comment period for residents on any city matters
Election of new chair and vice chair for the council
Fiscal year budget presentation and council input

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal disclosures about how residents can challenge city decisions. If you disagree with a city decision, you have 90 days from when that decision is final to file a lawsuit or legal challenge, or you'll lose your right to do so.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over decisions
Legal procedures for challenging city actions
California Civil Procedure Code requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026, to discuss updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030 and review requests from individual council members for new work projects. The council also considered changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled and approved meeting minutes and schedules.
keyTopics: Updated the 5-year federal funding and housing plan (Consolidated Plan)
Reviewed new project requests submitted by individual council members
Proposed changes to how council fills vacant seats
Approved meeting minutes and confirmed upcoming meeting dates

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to upgrade the building's historic status, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as a reward for preserving and improving the structure.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic safety improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule updates
March 12 meeting minutes approval
